message:
feat(net): CombinedWaker for owned TCP/Unix split halves and nonblocking enforcement

Redesign the owned split half architecture for TCP and Unix streams to
prevent lost wakeups when read and write halves are polled from different
async tasks.

net/tcp/split.rs:
- Add CombinedWaker struct implementing std::task::Wake that dispatches
  to per-direction wakers stored in a new SplitIoState
- Replace the bare Mutex<Option<IoRegistration>> in TcpStreamInner with
  Mutex<SplitIoState> holding registration + read_waker + write_waker
- When either half polls for I/O, it stores its own waker and registers
  a combined waker with the reactor, ensuring both halves are notified
  on any readiness event regardless of which task drives the poll
- Add corresponding test for concurrent half polling

net/tcp/stream.rs:
- Make from_std() return io::Result and call set_nonblocking(true) to
  prevent blocking mode inheritance from std sockets
- Add tcp_stream_from_std_forces_nonblocking_mode test using fcntl

net/tcp/listener.rs, socket.rs, traits.rs:
- Adjust Duration constants (from_secs → from_mins) and minor cleanups

net/unix/split.rs:
- Mirror the CombinedWaker + SplitIoState pattern from TCP for Unix
  stream owned split halves
- Add per-direction waker storage and combined waker dispatch

net/unix/stream.rs, listener.rs:
- Apply nonblocking enforcement and Duration constant improvements

net/dns/resolver.rs:
- Duration constant improvement (from_secs → from_mins)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
